english: of employing the (two) letters g and b Gram. Comp. and deriv. γnyis-skyes (gnyis skyes) one that is born twice i.e. a bird Cs.; also one that has entered into a religious order Cs. — γnyis-čár (gnyis char) v. čar (char). — γnyis-γnyis (gnyis gnyis) two a piece. — γnyis-ldáb (gnyis ldab) twofold, double, v. ldab (ldab). — γnyis-°túṅ (gnyis 'thung) (द्विप) 'drinking twice', the elephant. — γnyis-pa (gnyis pa) 1. the second. 2. having two, possessed of two, e.g. mgo-γnyis-pa (mgo gnyis pa) having two heads, two-headed; also double-tongued, deceitful W. 3. having doubts, doubting(?) Wdn. — γnyis-po (gnyis po) the two, both (v. above). — γnyis-méd (gnyis med) v. beginning of this article. — γnyis-°dzin (gnyis 'dzin) prob. the state of being affected or influenced by contrary things: doubt, unsteadiness, wavering Glr.; γnyis-°dzin ltá-ba (gnyis 'dzin lta ba) prob. to look upon two things as differing, to think them different Mil.

Cs. natural, opp. to bcós-ma (bcos ma) artificial, hence (Sch.) = dṅos-ma (dngos ma); Lexx. = निज innate, peculiar. It occurs in the expressions: sems γnyúg-ma (sems gnyug ma), and γnyúg-mai sems (gnyug mai sems) Mil; γnyúg-mai ye-śés (gnyug mai ye shes) Mil.; γnyúg-mai don (gnyug mai don) Mil. and Lex.; ma-bcós γnyúg-mai ṅaṅ-du °dres (ma bcos gnyug mai ngang du 'dres), perh.: is dissolved into the uncreated primordial existence Mil. Our Lama explained it differently in different passages, and was not certain of the true meaning of the word.
rape-seed for pressing oil; but cf. nyúṅ-ma (nyung ma).
Glr. also γnyeo (gnyeo), smyé-bo (smye bo), a wooer, courter.
the twisted part of the colon or great gut, Med. and col. (Sch. erron.: rectum).

resp. sku-γnyén (sku gnyen) 1. kinsman, relative, byáms-pai γnyen (byams pai gnyen) loving relations, frq.; γnyén-la byáms-pa byéd-pa (gnyen la byams pa byed pa) to love one's relatives; γnyén-gyi sgyúg-mo (gnyen gyi sgyug mo), sgyúg-mo (sgyug mo) as a degree of relationship Lex.; γnyen byéd-pa (gnyen byed pa) to become related, or allied, by marriage Dzl. — 2. gen. γnyén-po (gnyen po) helper, friend, assistant, esp. spiritually: rgyud γnyén-po bzáṅ-bar byín-gyis rlobs (rgyud gnyen po bzang bar byin gyis rlobs) bless my soul, that it may become a good spiritual helper (to these people) Mil.; γnyén-po-la ma ltós-par (gnyen po la ma ltos par) without looking up to a spiritual adviser Mil.; frq. used of supernatural helpers: bod °dúl-bai γnyén-po (bod 'dul bai gnyen po) the promoter of the conversion of Tibet (the special Saviour of Tibet, as it were), Awalokiteswara, frq.; applied to things: remedy, means, expedient, antidote, nád-la gsó-bar byéd-pai γnyén-po (nad la gso bar byed pai gnyen po) assistants in curing maladies (e.g. medicine, diet etc.) Med.; deí γnyén-por (dei gnyen por) as a remedy for Thgy., frq.; sgrúb-pai ṭabs mi śés-pai γnyén-por (sgrub pai thabs mi shes pai gnyen por) as a remedy for helplessness in acquiring a certain object, i.e. direction or instruction how to obtain it Thgy.; γnyén-po gsáṅ-ba (gnyen po gsang ba) mysterious helpers, or sources of good (relative to fetish-like objects frq.) — 3. Cs.: 'γnyén-po (gnyen po) adversary, antagonist, enemy; contrary, opposite, adverse'; Sch.: 'γnyén-por rtén-pa (gnyen por rten pa) to adhere to the counter party'; Lexx. have 'spáṅ-byai γnyén-po' (spang byai gnyen po) a γnyén-po (gnyen po) to be shunned, explaining γnyén-po (gnyen po) by प्रतिपक्ष (prob. to be corrected into पक्ष) opponent, adversary. Sure proofs of this signification of γnyén-po (gnyen po) I seldom met with in literature, but Lewin mentions some instances scarcely to be doubted. — 4. i. o. mnyen (mnyen) and bsnyen (bsnyen). Comp. dpuṅ-γnyén (dpung gnyen) helper, assistant, frq. — pá-γnyén (pha gnyen), má-γnyen (ma gnyen) a relation on the father's side, on the mother's side Cs. — bśes-γnyén (bshes gnyen) friend, esp. spiritual friend, v. bśes (bshes). — γnyen-grás (gnyen gras) (Sch.?), γnyen-°brél (gnyen 'brel), *nyen-ḍúṅ-po* (*nyen dung po* W.) relations, esp. of the same blood; γnyen-sdé (gnyen sde), γnyen-tsán (gnyen tshan), γnyen-sríd (gnyen srid) Mil. id., col. -- γnyen-°dún (gnyen 'dun) 1. Sch.: 'concord, harmony, amongst kinsmen', in which sense it seems to be used in Sty.: γnyen-°dún zád-pa yin (gnyen 'dun zad pa yin) this harmony ceases. 2. relations, ṕ́a yaṅ ma yin, γnyen-°dún min (pha yang ma yin, gnyen 'dun min) neither father nor relations Thgy. — γnyen-zlá (gnyen zla) prob.: qualified, fit for matrimonial alliance (as to birth etc.). kyéd-rnams kyaṅ ṅed rgya-nág-pai γnyen-zlá yin-pas (khyed rnams kyang nged rgya nag pai gnyen zla yin pas) as ye Tibetans may enter into connubial connexion with us Chinese Glr.; in a concrete sense: a good match. γnyen-zlá ma rnyéd- (gnyen zla ma rnyed-)
